2605.11290.title: ReAD: Reinforcement-Guided Capability Distillation for Large Language Models
2605.11290.paper_summary: Capability distillation applies knowledge distillation to selected model capabilities, aiming to compress a large language model (LLM) into a smaller one while preserving the abilities needed for a downstream task. However, most existing methods treat capabilities as independent training targets and overlook how improving one capability can reshape the student's broader capability profile, especially when multiple abilities jointly determine task success. We study capability distillation under a fixed token budget and identify two consistent patterns: distillation induces systematic, budget-dependent cross-capability transfer, and additional budget often brings limited task-relevant gains while sometimes degrading other useful abilities. Building on these insights, we propose ReAD, a Reinforcement-guided cApability Distillation framework that explicitly accounts for capability interdependence. ReAD first infers task-essential capabilities, then generates capability-targeted supervision on the fly, and finally uses an uncertainty-aware contextual bandit to adaptively allocate the distillation budget based on expected utility gains. Extensive experiments show that ReAD improves downstream utility under the same token budget while reducing harmful spillover and wasted distillation effort compared to strong baselines. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/LabRAI/ReAD.
2605.11290.paper_summary_zh: 能力蒸餾將知識蒸餾應用於選定的模型能力，旨在將大型語言模型（LLM）壓縮為更小的模型，同時保留下游任務所需的能力。<lb/>然而，大多數現有方法將能力視為獨立的訓練目標，並忽略了改善一項能力如何重塑學生的更廣泛能力配置，特別是當多種能力共同決定任務成功時。<lb/>我們在固定的標記預算下研究能力蒸餾，並確定了兩個一致的模式：蒸餾引發系統性的、依賴預算的跨能力轉移，並且額外的預算通常帶來有限的與任務相關的增益，同時有時會降低其他有用能力。<lb/>基於這些見解，我們提出了ReAD，一個強化引導的能力蒸餾框架，明確考慮能力之間的相互依賴性。<lb/>ReAD首先推斷任務所需的能力，然後即時生成針對能力的監督，最後使用一個考慮不確定性的上下文強盜來根據預期效用增益自適應分配蒸餾預算。<lb/>廣泛的實驗表明，與強基準相比，ReAD在相同的標記預算下提高了下游效用，同時減少了有害的溢出和浪費的蒸餾努力。<lb/>我們的代碼可在 https://github.com/LabRAI/ReAD 上公開獲取。
2605.11290.author: Xueqi Cheng et.al.
2605.11290.authors: Xueqi Cheng, Xugui Zhou, Tyler Derr, Yushun Dong
